summary: High-quality product-ideal blueprint describing an EC2-based swarm management system. Encodes a complete lifecycle state machine, a prioritized truth-reconciliation model, and operator-driven workflows (launch, hibernate, wake, terminate, image build, benchmark, dashboard) with strong causal chains. Semantic density is excellent—every line carries architectural signal. Minor issues: references Agentish as a string literal rather than the defined language constant, omits the reference-path directive, and some when/then chains (benchmark flow) pack many sequential steps that could benefit from intermediate state naming.
strengths: Truth hierarchy with explicit priority ordering (inventory > telemetry > history) is an elegant, recoverable pattern for reconciliation logic
Lifecycle model is comprehensive and forms a clear state machine—zombie detection conditions (inventory running + no telemetry + past grace window) demonstrate sophisticated failure-mode preloading
Zero waste: no comments, no filler phrases, no redundant declarations—every line advances the architectural description
Locally self-describing: a reader with only the language root can reconstruct the full system topology, actor roles, and operational workflows
Startup-path split (immutable vs mutable) cleanly separates baked-image concerns from runtime-refresh concerns at the right abstraction level
weaknesses: Missing the `/// <reference path="./_agentish.d.ts" />` directive that other files in the corpus use, breaking a small consistency convention
Agentish is referenced as the string `'Agentish'` in the blueprint format field rather than binding the language constant, losing the structural link to the language root
The benchmark when/then chain packs six sequential steps into a single causal block; intermediate lifecycle states would improve traceability for an implementing agent
No confirmable live-use evidence from the provided context—cannot verify whether this blueprint is actively paired with implementation code or operational tooling
